data.title: Expert Predicts Bitcoin Decline Amid Iran Conflict Impact on Oil Prices
data.description: Former hedge fund manager James Lavish has expressed concerns that Bitcoin may experience a further decline of 10%–20% if the ongoing conflict in Iran continues to exert pressure on oil prices. According to NS3.AI, Lavish highlighted that a prolonged conflict could lead to a new inflation shock, revive fears of stagflation, and necessitate a broader repricing across global markets.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | CFTC Reports Changes in COMEX Gold and Silver Positions
data.description: The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has released data indicating shifts in speculative positions for COMEX gold and silver as of the week ending March 31. According to Jin10, the net long positions for COMEX gold increased by 1,098 contracts, reaching a total of 93,872 contracts. In contrast, COMEX silver saw a reduction in net long positions by 99 contracts, bringing the total to 10,816 contracts.

data.title: HypurrFi Investigates Potential Domain Hijacking, Advises Users to Avoid Website and App
data.description: HypurrFi has issued a warning to its users, advising them to refrain from using its website or lending application due to an ongoing investigation into a potential domain hijacking. According to NS3.AI, the protocol currently holds approximately $30 million in total value locked. Despite the investigation, the team has assured users that there is no immediate risk to their funds. Additionally, HypurrFi's social media channels remain secure and under the team's control.

data.title: Digital Asset Flows Decline in Q1 2026, Driven by Corporate Treasury Purchases
data.description: JPMorgan analysts have reported that digital asset flows reached approximately $11 billion in the first quarter of 2026, marking a significant decrease compared to the same period last year. According to NS3.AI, the analysts attributed the majority of inflows to corporate treasury Bitcoin acquisitions and concentrated crypto venture capital funding, rather than contributions from retail or institutional investors. Additionally, the analysts noted a weakening in CME futures positioning and net outflows from spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs during the quarter, particularly in January.

data.title: Fidelity's Jurrien Timmer: Investors Shift Back to Bitcoin as Gold's Appeal Diminishes
data.description: Fidelity's Jurrien Timmer has observed a trend where investors who previously moved from Bitcoin to gold are now returning to Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, Timmer noted that gold's attractiveness is waning, while Bitcoin is stabilizing and gearing up for a possible upcoming rally.

data.title: Marathon Digital Holdings Reduces Workforce Amid Strategic Shift
data.description: Marathon Digital Holdings has announced a reduction of approximately 15% of its workforce following the sale of around 15,000 Bitcoin, valued at over $1.1 billion. According to NS3.AI, the company stated that this move is part of a strategic transition from solely focusing on Bitcoin mining to expanding into energy, digital infrastructure, and AI-related data centers. Marathon emphasized that the decision was driven by strategic considerations rather than financial necessity.

data.title: Expert Opinion: Michael Saylor Suggests Buying Bitcoin on Friday
data.description: Michael Saylor, a prominent figure in the cryptocurrency space, expressed his opinion that Friday presented a favorable opportunity to purchase Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, this statement was shared via Watcher.Guru's X account. Saylor's comments come amid ongoing discussions about the volatility and potential of Bitcoin in the financial markets.

data.title: U.S. Military Presence in Iran Likely as Oil Prices Fluctuate
data.description: Myriad traders have increased the likelihood of U.S. military deployment in Iran to 90% following reports of a U.S. fighter jet being shot down, which led to a rescue mission. According to NS3.AI, the market has also priced an 83% chance that West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil futures will reach $120 per barrel before dropping to $55.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Seeks Spot Bitcoin ETF with Competitive Fee
data.description: Morgan Stanley has submitted a filing for a spot Bitcoin ETF, proposing a 14 basis point fee, which is lower than BlackRock's 25 basis point charge for its IBIT product. According to NS3.AI, this move positions Morgan Stanley as the first major Wall Street investment bank to directly pursue a spot Bitcoin ETF. The proposed fund is pending regulatory approval before it can commence trading.

data.title: Iran Rejects Meeting with U.S. Officials, Criticizes Demands as Unacceptable
data.description: Iran has announced that it will not engage in discussions with U.S. officials, labeling the demands from the United States as unacceptable. According to NS3.AI, this statement was reported by Walter Bloomberg, highlighting the ongoing tensions between the two nations. The refusal to meet underscores the strained diplomatic relations and differing positions on key issues. Iran's stance reflects its dissatisfaction with the current U.S. approach, further complicating efforts to resolve longstanding disputes.

data.title: Elon Musk Urges Companies to Purchase Grok for SpaceX IPO Participation
data.description: Elon Musk has reportedly requested companies interested in participating in SpaceX's IPO to purchase Grok, according to the New York Times. Grok, an AI-driven platform, is seen as a strategic asset in Musk's broader vision for integrating advanced technologies into his ventures. According to Jin10, this move aligns with Musk's ongoing efforts to leverage AI capabilities across his business operations, enhancing efficiency and innovation. The SpaceX IPO is anticipated to be a significant event in the financial markets, drawing attention from investors worldwide. Musk's emphasis on Grok underscores the growing importance of AI in the tech industry, as companies seek to capitalize on its transformative potential.

data.title: Circle Faces Criticism Over Handling of Stolen USDC Transfers
data.description: Circle is under scrutiny following the movement of over $230 million in stolen USDC from Solana to Ethereum via the Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol (CCTP) during Drift Protocol's $285 million exploit. According to NS3.AI, the criticism has intensified as Circle froze the USDC balances of 16 unrelated corporate hot wallets on March 23 due to a civil dispute. Data from DeFiLlama indicates that Drift Protocol's total value locked (TVL) dropped to below $250 million after the attack.

data.title: Oxford Economics Predicts Federal Reserve Rate Cuts Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Oxford Economics has projected that the Federal Reserve might implement two rate cuts this year, despite facing an immediate inflation shock due to rising oil prices. According to NS3.AI, the firm highlighted that the U.S. employment figures for March may have exaggerated the labor market's strength before the onset of the Iran war. The ongoing conflict could potentially hinder hiring, spending, and investment activities in the upcoming months.

data.title: Oxford Economics: U.S. Employment Figures Overestimated Due to Iran War Impact
data.description: Oxford Economics reported on April 4 that the U.S. employment statistics for March significantly overestimated the pre-war strength of the job market. According to Jin10, this overestimation is due to declines in both the labor force and household employment numbers. The ongoing Iran war is affecting real economic activities, leading to a slowdown in employment growth. While the war's impact on inflation is immediate, its negative effects on consumer spending, business investment, and hiring will become more apparent in the coming months. Oxford Economics maintains its baseline forecast that the Federal Reserve will overlook the one-time shock of rising oil prices and proceed with two interest rate cuts this year to mitigate any potential weakness in the labor market.

data.title: Oxford Economics: The Federal Reserve may cut interest rates twice this year to support the labor market.
data.description: Oxford Economics states that the US March employment figures significantly overestimated the strength of the pre-war job market, as the data simultaneously reflected declines in both the labor force and household employment. Job growth will slow as the war in Iran impacts real economic activity. The war's impact on inflation is immediate, but its negative effects on consumer spending, business investment, and hiring will become more apparent in the coming months. Oxford Economics' baseline forecast remains that the Federal Reserve will ignore the one-off shock of rising oil prices this year and cut interest rates twice to guard against any future weakness in the labor market. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Judge Denies Rehearing in Federal Reserve Chair Powell Investigation
data.description: A U.S. judge has declined to rehear a case involving an investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. According to BlockBeats, the decision was made on April 4. The investigation had previously been concluded, and the request for a rehearing was denied, maintaining the original ruling.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 67,000 USDT with a Narrowed 0.06% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 03, 2026, 16:36 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 67,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 67,009.992188 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.06% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Unemployment Rate Emerges as Key Labor Market Indicator
data.description: The labor market no longer requires the creation of numerous jobs to maintain the same employment rate among the population. According to Odaily, in this context, monthly hiring data may not accurately reflect the health of the labor market. The unemployment rate could serve as a more effective measure. It is suggested that ratios and indicators such as the employment-to-population ratio, unemployment rate, quit rate, or hiring rate can better illustrate changes in the labor force size, providing a clearer picture of the labor market's health.

data.title: Amazon Shares Decline Amid Investor Concerns Over AI Investment Plans
data.description: Amazon shares experienced a 5.1% decline this week, contributing to an overall decrease of approximately 11% year-to-date. According to NS3.AI, this downturn reflects investor caution towards large-cap growth stocks. Despite Citi and JPMorgan increasing their price targets for Amazon to $285, market participants are primarily concerned about the company's planned $200 billion capital expenditure for AI by 2026 and its potential impact on future earnings.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Daly: Zero or negative job growth does not necessarily mean a weak economy.
data.description: Following the release of the non-farm payroll data, Federal Reserve Chairman Daly stated that zero or negative job growth does not necessarily indicate a weak economy; however, a slowdown in labor force growth does mean a slowdown in job creation. (Jinshi)
